summary: 7W / 8L across all 15 matchups (47% hit rate). The lone real stake — CHW @ TEX GOLD, an OVERRIDE of HARD CAP 7's bullpen-gap suppression justified by deGrom as an offsetting ace — lost 10-3 after deGrom himself gave up 5 ER in 4.2 IP, directly undercutting the override's own logic. More broadly, the model's 7 GOLD-tier picks went 4-3 (57%) pre-review, but Claude's DOWNGRADEs suppressed all four winners (BAL@BOS, NYM@MIL, WSH@COL, STL@LAA) while the one OVERRIDE landed on the loser — a net-negative day for Claude's tier calls despite individually defensible reasoning. The OAK@ARI rookie-SP DOWNGRADE was correctly protective.

MIN @ CLE.headline: CLE routed MIN 13-4; CONFIRM SKIP correctly avoided a lopsided loss on a marginal-favorite pick.
MIN @ CLE.hypothesis: HARD CAP 7 fired legitimately (hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=0.0242, above the sub-0.02 false-negative band) and CAP 10 zeroed the bottom-bucket marginal favorite (pick_prob=0.534). MIN getting blown out by 9 is a clean process success — the suppression was correctly scoped outside the disputed band.
MIN @ CLE.signals_to_recheck: None — HARD CAP 7 correctly fired outside the sub-0.02 false-negative band (gap=0.0242); CAP 10 bottom-bucket held as designed.

PIT @ NYY.headline: NYY beat PIT 8-5; a clean dual-opposing D-grade SKIP missed direction but sizing was correctly zero either way.
PIT @ NYY.hypothesis: SP-xERA and PQI both opposed NYY (grade D, score -2) yet NYY won anyway — another instance of dual-opposing missing direction while the suppression-only sizing rule held correctly (no stake regardless of outcome).
PIT @ NYY.signals_to_recheck: F1_xera_gap/pqi_diff dual-opposing — missed direction again (NYY won despite both opposing); reconfirms dual-opposing is stake-suppression only, not a fade.

TB @ TOR.headline: TB blew out TOR 7-1; CONFIRM SKIP protected against a lopsided loss on a Stage1/2-disagreement pick.
TB @ TOR.hypothesis: f5_full_delta=0.386 was the slate's largest Stage1/2 split (Cease F5-heavy lean vs a near-coinflip full game), and HARD CAP 1 already zeroed the negative -5.16pp edge. TOR's 6-run loss validates the suppression cleanly.
TB @ TOR.signals_to_recheck: f5_full_delta — a 0.386 Stage1/2 split correctly flagged instability; TOR's blowout loss supports treating large deltas as a stake-suppression trigger rather than a directional read.

BAL @ BOS.headline: BOS edged BAL 6-5; the starred-F1-without-F3 DOWNGRADE missed a real win for the first time in the tracked streak.
BAL @ BOS.hypothesis: Process-failure candidate. F1_xera_gap=1.39* with no F3 corroboration triggered a DOWNGRADE under a rule the archive had validated 4+ straight times (7/12-7/19), but BOS pulled out a genuine pre-cap A-grade win (score 5) by one run. SP-xERA independently agreed with the pick too, not just the starred F1 — the rule's 'no independent corroboration' framing may be too strict when SP-xERA and F1 both point the same direction even without F3.
BAL @ BOS.signals_to_recheck: F1_xera_gap* without F3_swing_take_gap corroboration — broke its win streak today (BOS won 6-5 after DOWNGRADE); archive moves to 4-for-6, no longer an automatic-DOWNGRADE-with-no-exceptions case.

LAD @ PHI.headline: PHI beat LAD 10-7; clean dual-opposing D-grade SKIP correctly called the loser.
LAD @ PHI.hypothesis: SP-xERA (0.69 against) and PQI (+14.4 against) both opposed LAD, and LAD lost by 3 — the opposing read was directionally right this time, consistent with dual-opposing splitting close to even rather than being a durable fade signal.
LAD @ PHI.signals_to_recheck: None — dual-opposing correctly predicted the loser here; sizing (SKIP) was correct regardless of direction.

SD @ ATL.headline: ATL edged SD 3-2; CONFIRM SKIP correct on a coin-flip edge outside the acceptable band.
SD @ ATL.hypothesis: Edge -8.31pp sat outside the [4,15]pp valid window and PQI leaned slightly against ATL, yet ATL won a one-run game — a genuinely marginal result consistent with the C-grade, score-0 read. No process failure either way.
SD @ ATL.signals_to_recheck: None — a one-run win on a coin-flip-graded pick is consistent with correctly-suppressed uncertainty, not a missed signal.

SF @ KC.headline: KC beat SF 4-3; DOWNGRADE of the PLATINUM-demoted GOLD pick was correct and protective.
SF @ KC.hypothesis: Already demoted PLATINUM->GOLD by the rolling-benchmark guardrail, then zeroed further by CAP 10's marginal-favorite rule and a 0.27 Stage1/2 disagreement. SF's narrow loss validates holding this at SKIP — a clean process success.
SF @ KC.signals_to_recheck: None — PLATINUM guardrail demotion plus HARD CAP 10 correctly suppressed a losing marginal favorite.

NYM @ MIL.headline: MIL crushed NYM 8-3; the negative-edge DOWNGRADE missed a 5-run GOLD win.
NYM @ MIL.hypothesis: Process-failure candidate. HARD CAP 1 correctly capped the -3.19pp edge on paper, but the underlying pre-cap A- stack (SP-xERA +2, PQI +16.2, Stage agree, score 4) was directionally strong and MIL delivered a 5-run win. Same shape as 7/18-7/19's CAP-1-zeroing-real-winners pattern (MIA@MIL, TB@BOS) — negative-edge GOLD picks with a strong pre-cap score keep winning despite the cap.
NYM @ MIL.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 1] negative-edge GOLD prevention — zeroed another strong pre-cap stack (score 4, A-) that went on to win by 5; joins TB@BOS/MIA@MIL (7/18-7/19) as evidence the cap may be too blunt when pre-cap score is 4+.

DET @ CHC.headline: DET beat CHC 8-6; dual-opposing D-grade SKIP correctly called the loser.
DET @ CHC.hypothesis: SP-xERA (1.18 against) and PQI (-11.0 against) both opposed CHC, and CHC lost — the opposing read was right this time, consistent with dual-opposing being roughly coinflip in aggregate but occasionally right.
DET @ CHC.signals_to_recheck: None — dual-opposing called this one correctly; sizing (SKIP) was correct regardless of direction.

CHW @ TEX.headline: CWS routed TEX 10-3 — Claude's only real stake of the day, an OVERRIDE of HARD CAP 7 suppression, was a clean process failure.
CHW @ TEX.hypothesis: Process failure. Claude overrode CAP 7's suppression on a +0.0077 bullpen gap, explicitly citing 'deGrom starting reduces reliance on the exact bullpen exposure the cap penalizes' as the offsetting factor — but deGrom himself was rocked for 5 ER in 4.2 IP and took the loss, directly contradicting the override's own rationale. The sub-0.02-band pattern itself still looks favorable in aggregate (6 of 7 tracked instances have now won, including today), but converting a population-level pattern into a single real stake carries real single-game variance, and the specific offsetting justification (an ace pitcher having a good day) was never independently checked against recent form.
CHW @ TEX.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 7] pick-side bullpen disadvantage — the override thesis took its first real-money loss (archive now 6-1 in the sub-0.02 band, still net-positive but no longer clean); deGrom's own poor start undercuts the ace-offsets-bullpen-exposure reasoning used to justify the override. Do not cite a starter's reputation as an offsetting factor without checking recent form/rest before converting a population-level pattern into a real stake.

MIA @ HOU.headline: HOU beat MIA 8-5; CONFIRM SKIP correct on the sp_savant_gate HARD_VETO for Blanco's thin current-season sample.
MIA @ HOU.hypothesis: Ronel Blanco's 0-Statcast-pitch gate correctly withheld a stake despite his career track record; HOU winning comfortably doesn't argue against the veto since it's a data-availability gate, not a quality read.
MIA @ HOU.signals_to_recheck: None — sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO functioned as designed on a thin-current-season-data case, independent of the directional win.

WSH @ COL.headline: WSH throttled COL 7-3; the triple-stacked DOWNGRADE (CAP1 + CAP10 + starred-F1-no-F3) missed a 4-run GOLD win.
WSH @ COL.hypothesis: Process-failure candidate, and paired with BAL@BOS makes the starred-F1-without-F3 rule 0-for-2 today. WSH's F1_xera_gap=1.50* had no F3 corroboration but did have F2_xwoba_gap and F5_bullpen_gap support, and the underlying pre-cap stack (score 4, A-) delivered comfortably. Combined with BAL@BOS, this is the clearest signal yet that the starred-F1-no-F3 auto-DOWNGRADE rule needs a secondary-signal carve-out (F2/F5 support) rather than treating F3 as the only valid corroboration.
WSH @ COL.signals_to_recheck: F1_xera_gap* without F3_swing_take_gap corroboration — second miss today (WSH won 7-3 after DOWNGRADE); both of today's misses (BAL@BOS, WSH@COL) had F2/F5 support even without F3 — consider whether F2_xwoba_gap or F5_bullpen_gap should count as valid secondary corroboration alongside F3.

OAK @ ARI.headline: OAK beat ARI 5-2; DOWNGRADE on the suspected CAP1 bypass plus rookie-SP blindspot was correct and protective.
OAK @ ARI.hypothesis: Process success. Mitch Bratt (2nd career MLB start) allowed 8 hits and 2 HR in 4.2 IP, validating the rookie-SP-blindspot flag independent of the suspected HARD CAP 1 code-path bypass (a negative-edge GOLD pick, edge_pp=-3.17, that reached Claude with no [HARD CAP 1] line in grade_reasons). Both reasons converged correctly on a loss.
OAK @ ARI.signals_to_recheck: small_sample_SP / rookie-SP blindspot (<6 career starts) — validated again (Bratt: 8 H, 2 HR allowed in 4.2 IP); also flag for engineering review — negative-edge GOLD (edge_pp=-3.17) reached Claude without a [HARD CAP 1] line in grade_reasons, a likely code-path gap distinct from the CAP 7 debate.

CIN @ SEA.headline: SEA shut out CIN 8-0; the slate's weakest D-grade stack (score -4) correctly called the loser by a wide margin.
CIN @ SEA.hypothesis: SP-xERA, PQI, and Stage1/2 disagreement all opposed CIN, plus fair_prob=0.397 sat below the 0.42 viability floor — every gate lined up against CIN and the 8-run shutout validates the read cleanly. Clean process success.
CIN @ SEA.signals_to_recheck: None — fair_prob<0.42 floor plus dual-opposing SP/PQI combined for the slate's most emphatic correct SKIP call.

STL @ LAA.headline: LAA edged STL 3-2; the DOWNGRADE Claude chose not to extend the CHW@TEX-style override to still missed a win, by the narrowest possible margin.
STL @ LAA.hypothesis: Genuinely mixed signal. Claude explicitly declined to override CAP 7 here (unlike CHW@TEX), citing a stacked bullpen_fatigue flag and a 15.06pp edge near the outer band with no offsetting ace factor — and LAA won by exactly 1 run, a margin consistent with real uncertainty rather than a clean miss. Combined with CHW@TEX, Claude's within-slate differentiation between two CAP-7-suppressed GOLD picks pointed the wrong way on both: the overridden game lost big, the left-suppressed game won narrow.
STL @ LAA.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 7] pick-side bullpen disadvantage — sub-0.02 band is now 6-1 across the July archive including today (STL@LAA won 3-2 while suppressed, CHW@TEX lost while overridden); the pattern is still net-positive but not clean enough to justify one-sided overriding without the >=0.02 threshold fix being backtested first.

patterns_observed: Claude's tier adjustments were net-negative today despite individually defensible reasoning: the model's 7 GOLD-tier picks went 4-3 (57%) pre-review, but Claude DOWNGRADEd 6 of the 7 to SKIP and OVERRODE the 7th (CHW@TEX) into a real stake — suppressing all 4 winners (BAL@BOS, NYM@MIL, WSH@COL, STL@LAA) while the sole real-money conviction landed on the one loser. Default posture on GOLD-tier picks should lean toward CONFIRM unless a specific rule has a strong recent track record; today shows that stacking several plausible-sounding suppression rules can collectively erase a tier that was actually beating coin-flip.

The starred-F1_xera_gap-without-F3-corroboration auto-DOWNGRADE rule went 0-for-2 today (BAL@BOS BOS won 6-5, WSH@COL WSH won 7-3), breaking a streak the operating brief described as '4+ consecutive validations' with 'no exception.' Archive moves to 4-for-6. Both of today's misses had F2_xwoba_gap or F5_bullpen_gap support even without F3 — recommend accepting F2/F5 as valid secondary corroboration alongside F3, and downgrading to half-Kelly rather than a full SKIP pending more data.

HARD CAP 7's sub-0.02 hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap band remains net-positive in aggregate (6 of 7 tracked instances won, including today's STL@LAA), but its first live conversion into a real stake (CHW@TEX OVERRIDE, citing deGrom as an offsetting ace) lost 10-3, and deGrom himself allowed 5 ER in 4.2 IP — directly falsifying the specific rationale used to justify the override. Keep tracking the band pattern for the proposed >=0.02 threshold fix, but stop using a starter's reputation alone as override justification; check recent form before staking real money on a population-level exception.

The rookie-SP blindspot flag (OAK@ARI, Mitch Bratt's 2nd career start: 8 H, 2 HR allowed in 4.2 IP) and the suspected HARD CAP 1 code-path bypass both correctly drove a protective DOWNGRADE on a losing pick — continue enforcing the rookie-SP flag as-is, and flag the CAP 1 grade_reasons gap (negative-edge GOLD reaching Claude uncapped) to engineering as a distinct bug from the CAP 7 threshold debate.
